Green-synthesized CQDs, with their unique properties and biocompatibility, offer a promising avenue. In the years to come, CQDs could play a crucial role in the development of novel antibacterial agents, particularly in the context of their green synthesis. Sustainable and eco-friendly CQD production methods using bio-derived precursors are expected to gain momentum, reducing the environmental impact associated with antibacterial agent production. Additionally, CQDs' versatile surface chemistry can be harnessed to design targeted antibacterial agents with reduced toxicity to human cells, a critical aspect in the fight against bacterial resistance. The future research agenda will likely focus on optimizing CQD-based antibacterial formulations, exploring their mechanisms of action, and enhancing their bioavailability. Green synthesis methods will ensure that these antibacterial agents are not only effective but also environmentally responsible, aligning with the global push for sustainable healthcare solutions. Collaboration between researchers, pharmaceutical companies, and healthcare institutions will be essential to expedite the translation of these green-synthesized CQDs into practical antibacterial treatments. In summary, the future and perspective of green CQD synthesis in antibacterial resistance are promising, as they provide a sustainable pathway to develop innovative antibacterial agents that can address this critical global health issue.
Overcoming the limitations of using CQDs in antimicrobial activities is crucial for their effective application in the future. Here are some potential solutions:

Enhanced Antibacterial Activity: To improve CQDs' antimicrobial effectiveness, researchers can focus on modifying their surface properties. Functionalizing CQDs with antibacterial agents, such as silver nanoparticles or specific organic molecules, can enhance their antibacterial activity. This can be achieved through a combination of green synthesis techniques and advanced surface modification methods. Targeted Delivery: Designing CQDs for targeted delivery to bacterial cells is essential. By attaching ligands or antibodies specific to bacteria on the CQD surface, researchers can ensure that the antimicrobial agents are delivered precisely to the pathogens, minimizing harm to healthy cells and reducing the required dosage. Combination Therapies: Combining CQDs with existing antibiotics or antimicrobial agents can be a potent strategy to overcome resistance issues. Synergistic effects can enhance the overall antimicrobial activity, potentially making it more difficult for bacteria to develop resistance. Optimization of Size and Structure: Fine-tuning the size and structure of CQDs can significantly impact their antibacterial properties. Research into the most effective CQD characteristics for different bacterial strains can lead to more tailored and efficient antimicrobial applications. Understanding Mechanisms of Action: A better understanding of how CQDs exert their antimicrobial effects is essential. By elucidating the mechanisms at the molecular level, researchers can design CQDs with even greater specificity and potency. Eco-Friendly Synthesis: Developing green and sustainable synthesis methods for CQDs will be crucial. Using biocompatible precursors and environmentally friendly processes will not only make CQD production more sustainable but also reduce potential harm to the environment and human health. Regulatory and Safety Considerations: Future research should also focus on safety aspects, including toxicity assessments, to ensure that CQD-based antimicrobial agents meet rigorous regulatory standards for human and environmental safety. Clinical Trials and Collaboration: To bring CQD-based antimicrobial solutions to the market, extensive clinical trials and collaborations between researchers, pharmaceutical companies, and healthcare institutions will be necessary. This will help validate the safety and efficacy of these novel treatments.

In conclusion, overcoming the limitations of using CQDs in antimicrobial activities requires a multidisciplinary approach, combining materials science, biology, and healthcare expertise. With innovative strategies, improved understanding, and a commitment to eco-friendly practices, CQDs have the potential to be powerful tools in combating antimicrobial resistance in the future.

A patent (CN113403069A) details the synthesis of a carbon quantum dot and a highly efficient antibacterial material that incorporates nano silver using the carbon quantum dot as a foundation. The patent also outlines a method for preparing this material. In contrast to previous findings, the carbon quantum dots facilitate the creation of a high-efficiency antibacterial composite material by interacting with silver nanoparticles. This composite material exhibits broad-spectrum antibacterial activity, particularly demonstrating remarkable effectiveness against antibiotic drug-resistant bacteria. The preparation method is straightforward, the raw materials are easily obtainable, and the process aligns with environmental protection requirements, making it suitable for mass production applications.

The patent identified as CN108300464B introduces a method for producing an antibacterial N-doped carbon quantum dot, along with its resulting product and potential applications. The antibacterial properties of these N-doped carbon quantum dots were evaluated using Escherichia coli. In comparison to alternative methods for preparing carbon quantum dots, this technique offers advantages such as simplicity, eliminating the need for additional surface passivators, rapid reaction rates, and the production of carbon quantum dots with strong fluorescence, excellent biocompatibility, and antibacterial efficacy. The prepared carbon quantum dots hold promising prospects for diverse applications in the biological and antibacterial fields.

The exploration of CQDs for investigating antibacterial resistance represents an exciting frontier in nanotechnology and biomedicine. The comprehensive review provided an in-depth understanding of CQDs' unique properties and their applications in tackling antibacterial challenges. Their excellent biocompatibility, tunable optical properties, and multifaceted mechanisms of antibacterial action have positioned them as promising candidates for a broad range of antibacterial applications. The classification of CQDs and their synthesis methodologies elucidated the versatility of these nanomaterials, while highlighting the importance of green synthesis methods in minimizing environmental impact and enhancing sustainability. The thorough characterization of CQDs, including their size, shape, crystal structure, and optical properties, is crucial for tailoring their attributes to specific applications. CQDs' exceptional antibacterial potential is underscored by their ability to generate ROS, disrupt bacterial membranes, and interfere with biofilm formation. These mechanisms make CQDs effective against diverse bacterial strains, including both Gram-positive and Gram-negative, offering promising solutions to the global challenge of antibacterial resistance. The results studies reinforce CQDs' suitability for antibacterial applications, with minimal cytotoxicity and exciting prospects for wound healing, photodynamic therapy, and other biomedical uses. The promising research endeavors highlighted throughout the review exemplify the growing significance of CQDs in addressing antibacterial resistance. The multifaceted nature of CQDs' antibacterial mechanisms and their eco-friendliness position them as vital contributors to future developments in antibacterial research. Continued exploration, optimization, and safety assessments will be essential to unlock their full potential, making them invaluable tools in combating bacterial infections and enhancing public health. Furthermore, the ongoing investigation into the hybrid and composite quantum dots offers the prospect of synergistic effects that can lead to even more potent antibacterial strategies. Ultimately, the future of CQDs in antibacterial research is bright, promising innovative and effective solutions to one of the most pressing global health challenges.
Not applicable.

ce:biography.ce:simple-para: Mohamed J. Saadh, a distinguished academic and researcher, is a prominent figure affiliated with the Faculty of Pharmacy at Middle East University in Amman, Jordan. With a strong commitment to advancing pharmaceutical sciences, Dr. Saadh has established himself as a key contributor to the academic and research landscape. Joining the faculty, Dr. Saadh's dynamic teaching style and interdisciplinary research have garnered recognition, making him a respected mentor and thought leader. His groundbreaking work in drug delivery systems and commitment to real-world applications continue to shape the future of pharmaceutical sciences, leaving an enduring legacy at Middle East University and within the broader scientific community.

F. Al-dolaimy, a distinguished scholar affiliated with Al-Zahraa University in Karbala, Iraq, has emerged as a trailblazer in the realm of interdisciplinary research. With a profound focus on the intersection of biology and materials science, Al-dolaimy has made substantial contributions to the understanding of cancer development and environmental monitoring. Moreover, Al-dolaimy's expertise extends to the realm of nanotechnology. This work highlights their commitment to leveraging cutting-edge technologies for the detection of marine toxins, addressing critical environmental concerns. Additionally, Al-dolaimy's research prowess is underscored in their research in that they explore innovative strategies for enhancing photocatalytic processes and wastewater treatment using advanced materials. Through their affiliation with Al-Zahraa University, F. Al-dolaimy continues to shape the academic landscape, bridging gaps between diverse scientific disciplines and fostering a legacy of impactful research.

Hassan Thoulfikar A. Alamir, an esteemed researcher associated with the Faculty of Pharmacy, Department of Pharmaceutics at the University of Al-Ameed in Iraq, stands at the forefront of pharmaceutical innovation. Alamir's scholarly pursuits focus on the intersection of cutting-edge technologies and medicinal applications, evident in his groundbreaking work. In his papers, Alamir showcases his expertise in biosensor development. This work underscores Alamir's commitment to advancing cancer diagnostics and treatment through innovative electrochemical approaches. Furthermore, Alamir's multidisciplinary approach is evident. Through a combination of experimental and theoretical investigations, Alamir contributes valuable insights into the potential anticancer and antibacterial properties of compounds, showcasing a holistic approach to pharmaceutical research. Affiliated with the University of Al-Ameed, Hassan Thoulfikar A. Alamir continues to make significant strides in the field of pharmaceutics, leaving an indelible mark on the scientific community with his dedication to advancing knowledge and improving healthcare outcomes.

Oras Kadhim, a distinguished researcher associated with Al-Manara College for Medical Sciences in Maysan, Iraq, has established himself as a prominent figure at the nexus of medical science and nanotechnology. With a keen focus on advancing healthcare solutions, Kadhim has made significant contributions to the understanding and management of diseases. His works reflect his comprehensive exploration of pathological conditions, demonstrating an in-depth knowledge of medical sciences. Furthermore, Kadhim's scholarly pursuits extend into the realm of nanotechnology, where he delves into the potential applications and implications of this cutting-edge field. Through his contributions to nanotechnology, Kadhim showcases a forward-thinking approach to medical research, aiming to harness the unique properties of nanomaterials for the development of innovative diagnostic and therapeutic strategies. Affiliated with Al-Manara College for Medical Sciences, Oras Kadhim continues to be a driving force in bridging the gap between traditional medical approaches and emerging technologies, leaving an impactful legacy in the pursuit of improved healthcare outcomes.

Salah Hassan Zain Al-Abdeen, a distinguished researcher affiliated with the Department of Medical Laboratories Technology at AL-Nisour University College in Baghdad, Iraq, has made significant strides at the intersection of materials science, catalysis, and computational chemistry. In his papers, Al-Abdeen demonstrates his expertise in the synthesis of advanced catalysts with a particular focus on their applications in photocatalysis and bactericidal processes. His research underscores a commitment to developing innovative solutions with potential biomedical implications. The computational studies showcases his proficiency in exploring the intricate dynamics of molecular systems under external stimuli, contributing valuable insights to the field of nanotechnology. Affiliated with AL-Nisour University College, Salah Hassan Zain Al-Abdeen continues to enrich the academic landscape through his multidisciplinary approach, bridging the gap between medical laboratories technology and cutting-edge advancements in materials and computational sciences.

Ruaa Sattar, an accomplished researcher affiliated with Al-Hadi University College in Baghdad, Iraq, has distinguished herself in the dynamic intersection of nanotechnology and antibacterial research. Through her multidisciplinary expertise, Sattar has significantly contributed to the field of nanotechnology with a focus on innovative applications for antibacterial purposes. Her work reflects a profound understanding of nanomaterials and their potential to combat bacterial infections, exemplified in her contributions to the field. Sattar's commitment to advancing knowledge in nano-enabled antibacterial strategies positions her as a leading figure in addressing critical challenges in healthcare. Affiliated with Al-Hadi University College, Ruaa Sattar continues to be at the forefront of research, bridging the gap between nanotechnology and antibacterial solutions, and leaving an indelible mark on the academic landscape with her dedication to improving public health outcomes.

Abeer Mhussan Jabbar, a distinguished researcher affiliated with the College of Pharmacy at the National University of Science and Technology in Dhi Qar, Iraq, has made significant contributions to the fields of pharmacology and microbiology. Jabbar explores her expertise in pharmacological research, particularly in the evaluation of the antioxidant properties of medicinal plants and their antibacterial performance. Her work reflects a dedication to exploring natural sources for potential therapeutic applications. Additionally, Jabbar extends her research prowess to the field of microbiology. Abeer Mhussan Jabbar, affiliated with the National University of Science and Technology, continues to play a pivotal role in advancing pharmaceutical and microbiological research, exemplifying a holistic approach to improving healthcare outcomes through her diverse scholarly pursuits.

Mohammed Kadhem Abid, a distinguished scholar affiliated with the Department of Anesthesia at the College of Health & Medical Technology, Al-Ayen University in Thi-Qar, Iraq, has showcased a versatile research portfolio spanning electrochemistry, nanochemistry, and biology. Abid in his papers reveals his prowess in materials science and sustainable energy, contributing to the development of efficient electro-catalysts for oxygen reduction in fuel cells. This work underscores his commitment to advancing green energy solutions. Additionally, Abid has delved into the realm of biology.

Raghu Jetti, a distinguished figure in the realm of biochemical and nanotechnological research, stands as a luminary in the academic corridors of the Department of Basic Medical Sciences at the College of Applied Medical Sciences, King Khalid University, Abha, Saudi Arabia. Driven by an insatiable curiosity and an unwavering commitment to advancing scientific knowledge, Jetti has dedicated his career to the intersection of biochemistry and nanotechnology. With an illustrious academic background and a wealth of research experience, he has contributed significantly to our understanding of the intricate molecular processes that underpin biological systems. His pioneering work in the application of nanotechnology to medical sciences has not only expanded the frontiers of knowledge but also holds immense promise for groundbreaking advancements in healthcare. As an esteemed member of the academic community, Raghu Jetti continues to inspire the next generation of scientists, fostering a culture of innovation and excellence in the pursuit of scientific discovery.

Ahmed Alawadi, an accomplished researcher and academician, is a distinguished figure in the field of environmental engineering and nanotechnology. Currently affiliated with the College of Technical Engineering at the Islamic University in Najaf, Iraq, Ahmed Alawadi has devoted his career to the development and investigation of innovative processes aimed at reducing pollutant emissions. His groundbreaking work focuses on the synthesis of nanocomposites, demonstrating a commitment to advancing sustainable solutions for environmental challenges. Through his research, Ahmed Alawadi has not only contributed significantly to the scientific community but has also played a pivotal role in addressing pressing environmental concerns, showcasing his dedication to the intersection of technology and environmental stewardship.

Ali Alsalamy, a distinguished researcher and academic, is affiliated with the College of Technical Engineering at Imam Ja’afar Al‐Sadiq University in Al‐Muthanna, Iraq. With a relentless passion for nanotechnology, Alsalamy has devoted his career to the synthesis and exploration of various nanocomposites. His groundbreaking work encompasses the development of nanofluids, core shell structures, and the investigation of their optical, sensor, and photocatalyst potentials. As a leading mind in the field, Alsalamy's contributions extend beyond the confines of traditional research, pushing the boundaries of nanoscience and technology. His commitment to advancing knowledge in these areas has not only elevated his own academic standing but has also played a pivotal role in shaping the landscape of nanomaterial research in Iraq and beyond.
